title: linaria glauca subsp. glauca
intro: Linaria glauca subsp. glauca, also known as the blue toadflax or glaucous linaria, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the family Plantaginaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. Some of the health benefits associated with Linaria glauca subsp. glauca include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and even support the treatment of certain types of cancer. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and can be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and health benefits.
